sprint_meta.notes: WK19 Round 1 — Agent 08 (UX/UI): hybrid Pygame overlay vs perspective camera (spec only)

round_meta.build_target: Specs & contracts — no implementation this round

prompt_text: Agent 08: Verify perspective camera shift does not break camera.ui full-screen Pygame overlay; guardrails; acceptance criteria and risks (Round 1 spec).

response.raw: Onboarded as Agent 08 (UX_UI_Director). Reviewed game/graphics/ursina_app.py: the hybrid UI is an Entity parented to camera.ui with model='quad', scale=(camera.aspect_ratio, 1), z=-1; each frame the Pygame framebuffer (engine.screen) is copied to Texture and assigned to ui_overlay.texture. In Ursina/Panda3D, camera.ui is the dedicated 2D screen-space overlay tree: children are laid out in UI/NDC-style space and are not transformed by the main 3D camera’s projection. Switching the scene camera from orthographic to perspective therefore should not, by itself, distort or “lose” the full-screen Pygame quad as long as it remains parented to camera.ui and scaling uses the current camera.aspect_ratio. Risks are mostly indirect: wrong parenting, stale aspect after resize, or future code attaching HUD elements to the 3D scene instead of camera.ui. UrsinaInputManager.get_mouse_pos currently returns (0,0) — perspective does not change that limitation, but when mouse is wired for clicks, coordinates must be mapped from window/UI space into the same pixel space as engine.screen for hit tests.
summary_bullets: camera.ui is screen-space overlay: 3D camera orthographic vs perspective does not apply to children of camera.ui.

Current overlay (ursina_app.py): Entity(parent=camera.ui, model='quad', scale=(camera.aspect_ratio, 1), z=-1); texture from pygame.image.tostring(engine.screen) — contract is sound for perspective world camera.
Guardrails: keep Pygame bridge quad strictly under camera.ui; refresh scale if aspect_ratio changes on resize; do not parent UI quads to scene or world entities.
Secondary Ursina Text in UrsinaRenderer (status line) should remain explicit parent=camera.ui (or Ursina default UI parent) so it is not confused with 3D world text.
Pre-existing gap: mouse position not yet mapped to pygame pixels — independent of perspective but blocks precise HUD clicking in Ursina mode.
proposed_changes: Round 2+ (implementation): When window resize is supported, set ui_overlay.scale = (camera.aspect_ratio, 1) (or equivalent) on resize so full-screen coverage stays correct.
Round 2+: Optionally document in ursina_app.py one-line comment that camera.ui ignores scene camera projection mode.
Coordinate with Agent 03: if any input pipeline maps window pixels to engine.screen, ensure same mapping after perspective (should be unchanged for overlay).
If Ursina Text widgets are added for debug, parent to camera.ui explicitly for consistent stacking with the Pygame quad.
acceptance_criteria: After perspective camera is enabled for the 3D scene, the Pygame-driven HUD/minimap/panels still fill the visible window with no letterboxing introduced solely by the camera mode change (same as pre-change behavior for a fixed window size).
ui_overlay remains a direct child of camera.ui with model quad; no regression in texture upload path (RGBA from pygame surface to Texture).
World camera pan/zoom/rotation (if any) does not change ui_overlay position, scale, or visibility except via intentional resize/aspect updates.
ESC/pause and other UI routes that depend on engine.render_pygame() still receive the same logical layout: layout is driven by surface.get_size() in game/ui, not by 3D projection.
Visual stacking: Pygame overlay remains the topmost full-screen UI layer (z ordering within camera.ui preserved relative to any other UI entities).
Regression check (manual, Ursina run): run with --ursina or equivalent entry, confirm no blank strips at edges and UI text readable at 1080p window.

risks.description: Mis-parenting: If future edits parent the Pygame quad to scene or to a 3D entity, it would inherit perspective transforms and break full-screen alignment.
risks.mitigation: Code review guardrail: only camera.ui for hybrid overlay; comment + optional assert in dev builds.

risks.description: Aspect ratio: scale uses camera.aspect_ratio at init only; window resize could desync quad coverage from viewport.
risks.mitigation: Hook window resize to update ui_overlay.scale; verify camera.aspect_ratio updates on Ursina window events.

risks.description: Input mapping: get_mouse_pos returns (0,0); perspective change does not fix this — click-through accuracy for HUD is still future work.
risks.mitigation: Track as separate UX task: map mouse.position/window size to engine.screen pixel coords for Ursina mode.

risks.description: Performance: full framebuffer to texture each frame may cost more at higher resolutions; unrelated to perspective but caps max FPS.
risks.mitigation: Monitor in Round 2+; consider dirty regions only if needed.

dependencies: Agent 03: UrsinaRenderer world coordinate contract (X/Z floor, Y height) — orthogonal to UI overlay; no change to pygame surface size contract expected.
Agent 09: volumetric entities in world space — must not draw UI in world space; art debug primitives stay under scene, not camera.ui.
questions_back_to_pm: Should WK19 include a minimal window-resize handler for the Ursina viewer, or defer to a follow-up round?
recommended_next_actions: Agent 08 (Round 2): After Agent 03 lands perspective camera in ursina_app.py, visually verify overlay full-bleed and document any resize hook.
Optionally file follow-up ticket: Ursina mouse pixel mapping for HUD hit testing.

agent_fields.verification_basis: game/graphics/ursina_app.py lines 52-60 (camera.ui quad), game/graphics/ursina_renderer.py Text overlay, game/ursina_input_manager.py get_mouse_pos
agent_fields.conclusion: Shifting the main scene camera to perspective should not break the camera.ui Pygame full-screen quad, provided overlay stays parented to camera.ui and aspect scaling stays in sync with the viewport.
